symptom: a child drooling and refusing food after swallowing something
hook: a perfectly round metal disc was lodged in the food pipe, facing flat to the camera
whatYouSee: A round, dense metallic disc sitting face-on in the oesophagus, typically at the thoracic inlet.
whyItMatters: A lodged coin can cause obstruction, drooling and aspiration; a button battery looks similar but has a double-ring halo and is a corrosive emergency.
treatment: An impacted oesophageal coin is removed endoscopically; a suspected button battery requires emergency removal because it burns tissue within hours.
takeaway: A round metal disc face-on in the food pipe is a swallowed coin, but always rule out a button battery.

condition.keyFindings: a round radiopaque metallic disc lying in the coronal plane (face-on) at the level of the thoracic inlet, distinguishing oesophageal from tracheal location

condition.takeaway: A round metal disc face-on in the food pipe is a swallowed coin, but always rule out a button battery.

generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Coin in the oesophagus

👀 What you see:
A round, dense metallic disc sitting face-on in the oesophagus, typically at the thoracic inlet.

🦴 Why it matters:
A lodged coin can cause obstruction, drooling and aspiration; a button battery looks similar but has a double-ring halo and is a corrosive emergency.

💊 Treatment:
An impacted oesophageal coin is removed endoscopically; a suspected button battery requires emergency removal because it burns tissue within hours.

📝 Takeaway:
A round metal disc face-on in the food pipe is a swallowed coin, but always rule out a button battery.
